options.name: <rect>
options.description: Specified rectangle to access values from

options.name: -area
options.description: Returns the area of the rectangle Example : db_rect -area {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 4.84

options.name: -center
options.description: Returns the center of the rectangle Example : db_rect -center {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 9.2 10.3

options.name: -length
options.description: Returns the larger of the -sizex and -sizey values Example : db_rect -length {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 2.2

options.name: -ll
options.description: Returns the lower left point of the rectangle Example : db_rect -ll {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 8.1 9.2

options.name: -llx
options.description: Returns the lower left x coordinate of the rectangle Example : db_rect -llx {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 8.1

options.name: -lly
options.description: Returns the lower left y coordinate of the rectangle Example : db_rect -lly {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 9.2

options.name: -size
options.description: Returns the size of the rectangle from lower left to upper right Example : db_rect -size {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 2.2 2.2

options.name: -sizex
options.description: Returns the x difference between the larger and smaller x values of the rectangle Example : db_rect -sizex {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 2.2

options.name: -sizey
options.description: Returns the y difference between the larger and smaller y values of the rectangle Example : db_rect -sizey {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 2.2

options.name: -ur
options.description: Returns the upper right point of the rectangle Example : db_rect -ur {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 10.3 11.4

options.name: -urx
options.description: Returns the upper right x coordinate of the rectangle Example : db_rect -urx {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 10.3

options.name: -ury
options.description: Returns the upper right y coordinate of the rectangle Example : db_rect -ury {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 11.4

options.name: -width
options.description: Returns the smaller of the -sizex and -sizey values Example : db_rect -width {8.1 9.2 10.3 11.4} returns 2.2
